scopeFiles: apps/api/main.py (EDIT — provider configs + active-selection persistence and endpoints; /chat uses active provider/model; key masking)
apps/api/test_main.py (EDIT — providers CRUD/persistence + stub-provider selection tests + masking)
apps/web/components/ (EDIT/NEW — provider switcher in chat header; settings form components)
.env.example (EDIT — document provider options; note keys live in local data, not committed)
successCriteria: In Settings, a user can add at least one provider config (label, base URL, model, API key), see it listed, edit and delete it, and mark one active; the active selection persists across reloads because it is served from the backend.
Stored API keys are never returned in full by the API (masked on read) and never written to a git-tracked file.

The chat header shows the active provider/model and lets the user switch it; sending a chat uses the selected provider/model — verifiable because the outbound request to the stubbed provider carries the selected base URL and model.

The backend exposes providers/settings CRUD + active-selection endpoints backed by SQLite in the gitignored data dir, and /chat resolves the active provider/model with environment as a default fallback.

Automated pytest covers providers CRUD + active persistence round-trip, /chat streaming using the selected provider/model against a stub provider (no network/secrets), and key masking — and all tests pass.
next build passes and the existing chat + session behavior still works.
.nogra/state/SESSION-CHECKPOINT.md and CURRENT-TASKS.md are updated to mark Phase 2a done and name Phase 2b (Notes/Tasks/Documents) and Phase 3 (Memory/RAG).
stopCriteria: First action: read apps/web/AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md and .nogra/state/*; then run pre-flight checks: `node --version`, `npm --version`, and `py -3.11 --version` (or `python --version`). If Node, npm, or Python 3.11+ is missing, status: blocked, name what is missing, and do not install runtimes or scaffold partial files.
Before writing any frontend code, read the relevant guides under apps/web/node_modules/next/dist/docs/ for Next.js 16 / React 19 / Tailwind v4 conventions; do not rely on pre-Next-15 assumptions.
Do not add real API keys or secrets to the repo and do not call live external providers, including in tests — use a stub/fake endpoint. Keys persist only in the gitignored local data dir.

Do not implement Phase 2b modules (real Notes/Tasks/Documents features), Memory/RAG, auth, or deployment; if delivering the selector would require going outside this slice, stop and return for a follow-up brief.
If `next build` or `pytest` exits non-zero and cannot proceed cleanly, stop with the command output rather than hand-patching a broken state.
Do not modify .nogra/ contracts or config.json; only update the named .nogra/state/* continuity files.

summary: Phase 2a (multi-provider selector) delivered and independently Manager-verified on Windows: 11/11 pytest green, next build clean. Backend providers CRUD + active selection in SQLite (keys masked, apps/api/lantern.db gitignored), /chat resolves active provider with env fallback; Settings UI + chat-header switcher added. 6/7 brief criteria met by the executor; criterion #7 (.nogra/state) completed by the Manager after the executor ran out of turns.
